query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "memlens: benchmarking multimodal long-term memory in large vision-lang" computer science
rationale: The paper is a general review of object detection using LVLMs and does not address the specific mechanism of "multimodal long-term memory" or the "MemLens" benchmarking framework central to the user's question. While it shares the broad domain of LVLMs, it fails to satisfy criteria (a) or (b) because it does not measure memory retention, recall over time, or the specific variables associated with long-term memory benchmarking, instead focusing on detection accuracy and localization.
